        (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, '1.0\xa0TB'), (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, '1.0\xa0PB'),
        (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 2000, '2000.0\xa0PB'), (complex(1, -1), '0\xa0bytes'), ('', '0\xa0bytes'), ('α', '0\xa0bytes')
    ]
    tests_localized = [
        (0, '0\xa0Bytes'), (1, '1\xa0Byte'), (1023, '1023\xa0Bytes'), (1024, '1,0\xa0KB'),
        (10 * 1024, '10,0\xa0KB'), (1024 * 1024 - 1, '1024,0\xa0KB'), (1024 * 1024, '1,0\xa0MB'),
        (1024 * 1024 * 50, '50,0\xa0MB'), (1024 * 1024 * 1024 - 1, '1024,0\xa0MB'), (1024 * 1024 * 1024, '1,0\xa0GB'),
        (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, '1,0\xa0TB'), (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, '1,0\xa0PB'),
        (1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 2000, '2000,0\xa0PB'), (complex(1, -1), '0\xa0Bytes'), ('', '0\xa0Bytes'), ('α', '0\xa0Bytes')
    ]

    for value, expected in tests_default:
        with self.subTest(test='default', value=value):
            self.assertEqual(filesizeformat(value), expected)

    with translation.override('de'):
        for value, expected in tests_localized:
            with self.subTest(test='localized', value=value):
                self.assertEqual(filesizeformat(value), expected)

    both localized and non-localized.

    test_cases = [
        {
            "field_kwargs": {"input_formats": ['%H.%M.%S %m.%d.%Y', '%H.%M %m-%d-%Y']},
            "invalid_inputs": ['2010-12-21 13:30:05 13:30:05', '1:30:05 PM 21/12/2010', '13:30:05 21.12.2010'],
            "valid_inputs": [
                ('13.30.05 12.21.2010', datetime(2010, 12, 21, 13, 30, 5), '21.12.2010 13:30:05'),
                ('13.30 12-21-2010', datetime(2010, 12, 21, 13, 30), '21.12.2010 13:30:00'),
            ],
            "extra_valid": []
        },
        {
            "field_kwargs": {"input_formats": ['%H.%M.%S %m.%d.%Y', '%H.%M %m-%d-%Y'], "localize": True},
            "invalid_inputs": ['2010/12/21 13:30:05', '1:30:05 PM 21/12/2010', '13:30:05 21.12.2010'],
            "valid_inputs": [
                ('13.30.05 12.21.2010', datetime(2010, 12, 21, 13, 30, 5), '21.12.2010 13:30:05'),
                ('13.30 12-21-2010', datetime(2010, 12, 21, 13, 30), '21.12.2010 13:30:00'),
            ],
            "extra_valid": [
                ('2010-12-21 13:30:05', datetime(2010, 12, 21, 13, 30, 5))
            ]
        }
    ]

    for case in test_cases:
        with self.subTest(localize=case["field_kwargs"].get("localize", False)):
            f = forms.DateTimeField(**case["field_kwargs"])
            for invalid_input in case["invalid_inputs"]:
                with self.subTest(invalid_input=invalid_input):
                    with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):
                        f.clean(invalid_input)
            for input_str, expected_dt, expected_text in case["valid_inputs"]:
                with self.subTest(valid_input=input_str):
                    result = f.clean(input_str)
                    self.assertEqual(result, expected_dt)
                    text = f.widget.format_value(result)
                    self.assertEqual(text, expected_text)
            for input_str, expected_dt in case["extra_valid"]:
                with self.subTest(extra_valid_input=input_str):
                    result = f.clean(input_str)
                    self.assertEqual(result, expected_dt)

@skipUnlessDBFeature('supports_table_check_constraints')
def test_constraint_percent_escaping(self):
    app_label = 'add_constraint_string_quoting'
    checks = [
        (models.Q(name__startswith='Albert'), {'name': 'Alberta'}, {'name': 'Artur'}),
        (models.Q(rebate__endswith='%'), {'rebate': '10%'}, {'rebate': '10%$'}),
        (~models.Q(surname__startswith=models.F('name')), {'name': 'Albert'}, {'name': 'Albert', 'surname': 'Alberto'}),
        (models.Q(name='%'), {'name': '%'}, {'name': 'Albert'}),
    ]

    # Test both creating model with constraint and adding constraint to existing model
    for operation_type in ('create', 'add'):
        with self.subTest(operation=operation_type):
            if operation_type == 'create':
                from_state = ProjectState()
            else:
                operations = [
                    migrations.CreateModel(
                        'Author',
                        fields=[
                            ('id', models.AutoField(primary_key=True)),
                            ('name', models.CharField(max_length=100)),
                            ('surname', models.CharField(max_length=100), default='' if operation_type == 'add' else models.F('')),
                            ('rebate', models.CharField(max_length=100)),
                        ],
                    )
                ]
                from_state = self.apply_operations(app_label, ProjectState(), operations)

            for check, valid, invalid in checks:
                with self.subTest(condition=check, valid=valid, invalid=invalid):
                    constraint = models.CheckConstraint(condition=check, name='constraint')
                    if operation_type == 'create':
                        operation = migrations.CreateModel(
                            'Author',
                            fields=[
                                ('id', models.AutoField(primary_key=True)),
                                ('name', models.CharField(max_length=100)),
                                ('surname', models.CharField(max_length=100, db_default='')),
                                ('rebate', models.CharField(max_length=100)),
                            ],
                            options={'constraints': [constraint]},
                        )
                    else:
                        operation = migrations.AddConstraint('Author', constraint)

                    to_state = from_state.clone()
                    operation.state_forwards(app_label, to_state)
                    with connection.schema_editor() as editor:
                        operation.database_forwards(app_label, editor, from_state, to_state)

                    Author = to_state.apps.get_model(app_label, 'Author')
                    try:
                        with transaction.atomic():
                            Author.objects.create(**valid).delete()
                        with self.assertRaises(IntegrityError), transaction.atomic():
                            Author.objects.create(**invalid)
                    finally:
                        with connection.schema_editor() as editor:
                            if operation_type == 'create':
                                migrations.DeleteModel('Author').database_forwards(app_label, editor, to_state, from_state)
                            else:
                                operation.database_backwards(app_label, editor, from_state, to_state)

def test_anonymous_and_inactive_have_no_permissions(self):
    """
        #17903 -- Anonymous and inactive users shouldn't have permissions in
        ModelBackend.get_(all|user|group)_permissions().
    """
    backend = ModelBackend()
    user = self.UserModel._default_manager.get(pk=self.user.pk)
    content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(Group)
    user_perm = Permission.objects.create(name='test', content_type=content_type, codename='test_user')
    group_perm = Permission.objects.create(name='test2', content_type=content_type, codename='test_group')
    user.user_permissions.add(user_perm)
    group = Group.objects.create(name='test_group')
    user.groups.add(group)
    group.permissions.add(group_perm)

    self.assertEqual(backend.get_all_permissions(user), {'auth.test_user', 'auth.test_group'})
    self.assertEqual(backend.get_user_permissions(user), {'auth.test_user'})
    self.assertEqual(backend.get_group_permissions(user), {'auth.test_group'})

    for condition, context in (
        ("anonymous", mock.patch.object(self.UserModel, 'is_anonymous', True)),
        ("inactive", None),
    ):
        with self.subTest(condition=condition):
            if condition == "inactive":
                user.is_active = False
                user.save()
            else:
                context.__enter__()
            try:
                self.assertEqual(backend.get_all_permissions(user), set())
                self.assertEqual(backend.get_user_permissions(user), set())
                self.assertEqual(backend.get_group_permissions(user), set())
            finally:
                if condition == "anonymous":
                    context.__exit__(None, None, None)

def test_directory_times_changing_after_file_operations(self):
    """
    Directory modified and accessed time should change when a file is created or deleted inside,
    but the created time should remain the same.
    """
    operations = [
        ('create', lambda: self.storage.save('dir/file2.txt', ContentFile('test'))),
        ('delete', lambda: self.storage.delete('dir/file.txt'))
    ]

    # Prepare initial file for delete operation
    self.storage.save('dir/file.txt', ContentFile('test'))

    for operation_name, operation in operations:
        with self.subTest(operation=operation_name):
            created_time = self.storage.get_created_time('dir')
            modified_time = self.storage.get_modified_time('dir')
            accessed_time = self.storage.get_accessed_time('dir')
            time.sleep(0.1)
            operation()
            new_modified_time = self.storage.get_modified_time('dir')
            new_accessed_time = self.storage.get_accessed_time('dir')
            after_operation_created_time = self.storage.get_created_time('dir')

            self.assertGreater(new_modified_time, modified_time)
            self.assertGreater(new_accessed_time, accessed_time)
            self.assertEqual(created_time, after_operation_created_time)
```

def test_health_checks(self):

        with self.subTest(conn_health_checks=enabled):
            self.patch_settings_dict(conn_health_checks=enabled)
            self.assertIsNone(connection.connection)
            with patch.object(connection, 'is_usable', side_effect=AssertionError):
                self.run_query()
            old_connection = connection.connection
            connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()
            self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)
            if enabled:
                with patch.object(connection, 'is_usable', return_value=False) as mocked_is_usable:
                    self.run_query()
                    new_connection = connection.connection
                    self.assertIsNot(new_connection, old_connection)
                    self.run_query()
                    self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)
                self.assertEqual(mocked_is_usable.call_count, 1)
                connection.close_if_unusable_or_obsolete()
                self.run_query()
                self.run_query()
                self.assertIs(new_connection, connection.connection)
            else:
                with patch.object(connection, 'is_usable', side_effect=AssertionError):
                    self.run_query()
                    self.assertIs(old_connection, connection.connection)

from django.test import override_settings
from django.db.migrations.loader import MigrationLoader
from django.db.migrations.recorder import MigrationRecorder
from django.db.migrations.exceptions import NodeNotFoundError

class LoaderTests(TestCase):

    def record_applied(self, recorder, app_label, name):
        recorder.record_applied(app_label, name)

    def run_migration_plan_test(self, migration_module, expected_results, error_cases=None):
        with override_settings(MIGRATION_MODULES={'migrations': migration_module}):
            loader = MigrationLoader(connection)
            recorder = MigrationRecorder(connection)
            self.addCleanup(recorder.flush)

            def num_nodes():
                plan = set(loader.graph.forwards_plan(('migrations', '7_auto')))
                return len(plan - loader.applied_migrations.keys())

            for step in expected_results:
                with self.subTest(migration=step['migration']):
                    if 'error_msg' in step:
                        self.record_applied(recorder, 'migrations', step['migration'])
                        with self.assertRaisesMessage(NodeNotFoundError, step['error_msg']):
                            loader.build_graph()
                    else:
                        self.record_applied(recorder, 'migrations', step['migration'])
                        loader.build_graph()
                        self.assertEqual(num_nodes(), step['expected_num_nodes'])

    def test_loading_squashed_migrations(self):
        """Tests loading complex squashed migrations, both normal and erroneous"""

        # Test case 1: complex
        expected_results_complex = [
            {'migration': None, 'expected_num_nodes': 5},
            {'migration': '1_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 4},
            {'migration': '2_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 3},
            {'migration': '3_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 4},
            {'migration': '4_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 3},
            {'migration': '5_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 2},
            {'migration': '6_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 1},
            {'migration': '7_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 0},
        ]
        # Adjust first step to build graph without recording any applied migration
        # So we handle it specially in run_migration_plan_test by starting with build_graph before loop
        def run_complex():
            with override_settings(MIGRATION_MODULES={'migrations': 'migrations.test_migrations_squashed_complex'}):
                loader = MigrationLoader(connection)
                recorder = MigrationRecorder(connection)
                self.addCleanup(recorder.flush)

                def num_nodes():
                    plan = set(loader.graph.forwards_plan(('migrations', '7_auto')))
                    return len(plan - loader.applied_migrations.keys())

                loader.build_graph()
                self.assertEqual(num_nodes(), 5)

                for step in expected_results_complex[1:]:
                    with self.subTest(migration=step['migration']):
                        self.record_applied(recorder, 'migrations', step['migration'])
                        loader.build_graph()
                        self.assertEqual(num_nodes(), step['expected_num_nodes'])

        run_complex()

        # Test case 2: erroneous
        error_msg = ("Migration migrations.6_auto depends on nonexistent node ('migrations', '5_auto'). "
                     "Django tried to replace migration migrations.5_auto with any of [migrations.3_squashed_5] "
                     "but wasn't able to because some of the replaced migrations are already applied.")
        expected_results_erroneous = [
            {'migration': None, 'expected_num_nodes': 5},
            {'migration': '1_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 4},
            {'migration': '2_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 3},
            {'migration': '3_auto', 'error_msg': error_msg},
            {'migration': '4_auto', 'error_msg': error_msg},
            {'migration': '5_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 2},
            {'migration': '6_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 1},
            {'migration': '7_auto', 'expected_num_nodes': 0},
        ]

        def run_erroneous():
            with override_settings(MIGRATION_MODULES={'migrations': 'migrations.test_migrations_squashed_erroneous'}):
                loader = MigrationLoader(connection)
                recorder = MigrationRecorder(connection)
                self.addCleanup(recorder.flush)

                def num_nodes():
                    plan = set(loader.graph.forwards_plan(('migrations', '7_auto')))
                    return len(plan - loader.applied_migrations.keys())

                loader.build_graph()
                self.assertEqual(num_nodes(), 5)

                for step in expected_results_erroneous[1:]:
                    with self.subTest(migration=step['migration']):
                        if 'error_msg' in step:
                            self.record_applied(recorder, 'migrations', step['migration'])
                            with self.assertRaisesMessage(NodeNotFoundError, step['error_msg']):

                        else:

                            loader.build_graph()
                            self.assertEqual(num_nodes(), step['expected_num_nodes'])

        run_erroneous()

def test_head_and_get_requests(self):
    hello_world_body = b'<!DOCTYPE html><html><body>Hello World</body></html>'
    content_length = len(hello_world_body)

    def test_app_no_content_length(environ, start_response):
        """A WSGI app that returns a hello world without Content-Length header."""
        start_response('200 OK', [])
        return [hello_world_body]

    def test_app_with_content_length(environ, start_response):
        """A WSGI app that returns a hello world with non-zero Content-Length."""
        start_response('200 OK', [('Content-length', str(content_length))])
        return [hello_world_body]

    test_cases = [
        {
            'method': b'GET',
            'app': test_app_no_content_length,
            'expected_body': hello_world_body,
            'expected_content_length': f'Content-Length: {content_length}\r\n'.encode(),
            'expect_content_length_header': True,
        },
        {
            'method': b'HEAD',
            'app': test_app_no_content_length,
            'expected_body': b'\r\n',
            'expected_content_length': None,
            'expect_content_length_header': False,
        },
        {
            'method': b'HEAD',
            'app': test_app_with_content_length,
            'expected_body': b'\r\n',
            'expected_content_length': f'Content-length: {content_length}\r\n'.encode(),
            'expect_content_length_header': True,
        },
    ]

    for case in test_cases:
        with self.subTest(method=case['method'], app=case['app']):
            rfile = BytesIO(case['method'] + b' / HTTP/1.0\r\n')
            rfile.seek(0)
            wfile = UnclosableBytesIO()

            def makefile(mode, *a, **kw):
                if mode == 'rb':
                    return rfile
                elif mode == 'wb':
                    return wfile

            request = Stub(makefile=makefile)
            server = Stub(base_environ={}, get_app=lambda: case['app'])
            with self.assertLogs('django.server', 'INFO'):
                WSGIRequestHandler(request, '192.168.0.2', server)
            wfile.seek(0)
            lines = list(wfile.readlines())
            body = lines[-1]
            self.assertEqual(body, case['expected_body'])
            if case['expect_content_length_header']:
                self.assertIn(case['expected_content_length'], lines)
            else:
                self.assertFalse(any(line.startswith(b'Content-Length:') or line.startswith(b'Content-length:') for line in lines))
            self.assertNotIn(b'Connection: close\r\n', lines)

            result = self.get_runner()._makeResult()

            self.assertEqual(len(result.errors), 1)
            _, traceback = result.errors[0]

            _pre_setup.assert_called_once_with()
            if post_teardown_called:

            else:

            # Reset mocks for next subTest

def test_difference_and_symdifference(self):
    """Testing difference() and sym_difference()."""
    for i in range(len(self.geometries.topology_geoms)):
        with self.subTest(i=i, method="difference"):
            a = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.topology_geoms[i].wkt_a)
            b = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.topology_geoms[i].wkt_b)
            d1 = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.diff_geoms[i].wkt)
            d2 = a.difference(b)
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals(d2.geos))
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals((a - b).geos))
            a -= b
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals(a.geos))

        with self.subTest(i=i, method="sym_difference"):
            a = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.topology_geoms[i].wkt_a)
            b = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.topology_geoms[i].wkt_b)
            d1 = OGRGeometry(self.geometries.sdiff_geoms[i].wkt)
            d2 = a.sym_difference(b)
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals(d2.geos))
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals((a ^ b).geos))
            a ^= b
            self.assertTrue(d1.geos.equals(a.geos))

def test_override_combined(self):
    default = timezone.get_default_timezone()

    @timezone.override(EAT)
    def func_tz_eat():
        self.assertIs(EAT, timezone.get_current_timezone())

    @timezone.override(None)
    def func_tz_none():
        self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())

    try:
        timezone.activate(ICT)

        with self.subTest("context manager override with EAT"):
            with timezone.override(EAT):
                self.assertIs(EAT, timezone.get_current_timezone())
            self.assertIs(ICT, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("context manager override with None"):
            with timezone.override(None):
                self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())
            self.assertIs(ICT, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("decorator override with EAT"):
            func_tz_eat()
            self.assertIs(ICT, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("decorator override with None"):
            func_tz_none()
            self.assertIs(ICT, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        timezone.deactivate()

        with self.subTest("context manager override with EAT after deactivate"):
            with timezone.override(EAT):
                self.assertIs(EAT, timezone.get_current_timezone())
            self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("context manager override with None after deactivate"):
            with timezone.override(None):
                self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())
            self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("decorator override with EAT after deactivate"):
            func_tz_eat()
            self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())

        with self.subTest("decorator override with None after deactivate"):
            func_tz_none()
            self.assertIs(default, timezone.get_current_timezone())

    finally:
        timezone.deactivate()
```

            result = f.clean('13:30')
            self.assertEqual(result, time(13, 30, 0))

            self.assertEqual(text, '13:30:00')
